objective: Measure whether Qwen3.5-4B held-out coding failures are diversity-limited or capability-limited before training any diversity-keyed adapter.
primary_question: Among held-out MBPP tasks with no hidden-correct candidate in a small base pool, how many become covered by higher-K sampling and tuned diverse decoding at matched task prompts?

planned_stages.name: smoke
planned_stages.purpose: Validate sampling, execution, per-test functional signatures, manifests, and runtime.

planned_stages.name: base_pool
planned_stages.purpose: Sample a small base pool to identify zero-coverage held-out tasks.

planned_stages.name: zero_base_ladder
planned_stages.purpose: Spend extra sampling budget only on base-missed tasks under default/hot/diverse decoding arms.

planned_stages.name: combined_inference
planned_stages.purpose: Run frozen self-repair on the strongest sampled pool's remaining failures.

main_metrics: functional diversity from per-test failure bitstrings
false repair rate for visible-passing repairs

gates.diversity_training_positive_gate: High-K/tuned sampling recovers a meaningful fraction of base-missed tasks; otherwise do not train strategy-token LoRA.
gates.combined_recipe_gate: Frozen repair adds coverage on tasks not recovered by sampling without excessive false repairs.

artifact_policy.large_files: No model checkpoints expected for the gated diagnostic; large files must stay out of the experiment directory.
